description: Dose metrics, DVH analysis, plan quality

cases.input: Please evaluate the dose distribution for a completed prostate LDR brachytherapy plan. The DVH shows D90 = 148 Gy, V100 = 94%, V150 = 52%, V200 = 18%. The rectal D1cc is 125 Gy and the urethral Dmax is 168 Gy. Evaluate these parameters against the ABS quality guidelines and provide a comprehensive assessment.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: DVH D90 V100 V150 rectal D1cc urethral Dmax ABS guidelines

cases.input: I have a post-implant CT scan for a prostate I-125 seed implant. The dosimetric evaluation shows D90 = 135 Gy, V100 = 88%, V150 = 65%, and VC150 = 45%. The prostate volume edema was 30% on day 1 and has resolved to baseline by day 30. Evaluate whether the plan meets quality benchmarks and discuss the impact of edema resolution on the final dosimetry.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: post-implant D90 V100 edema resolution quality benchmark

cases.input: I need to evaluate the dose to the femoral heads for a prostate brachytherapy case. The right femoral head Dmax is 82 Gy and the left femoral head Dmax is 95 Gy. The patient had a prior hip replacement on the left. Evaluate the clinical significance of these doses and recommend whether any corrective action is needed.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: femoral head dose prostate asymmetric hip replacement

cases.input: Please evaluate the DVH parameters for a breast APBI case using MammoSite. The PTV D90 is 33.2 Gy, V90 is 92%, and the skin Dmax is 34.8 Gy. The chest wall dose is minimal. Evaluate whether these parameters meet the ABS and ASTRO consensus guidelines for APBI and identify any concerns.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: breast APBI PTV D90 V90 skin Dmax ABS ASTRO MammoSite

cases.input: I have a post-implant dosimetry report for a Pd-103 prostate implant. The D90 is 98 Gy, V100 is 92%, and V150 is 55%. The prostate volume was 45cc and 85 seeds were implanted. Evaluate the implant quality and compare the results with published benchmarks for Pd-103 implants.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: Pd-103 prostate D90 V100 V150 implant quality benchmark

cases.input: Please evaluate the dose distribution for an endometrial cancer vaginal cuff brachytherapy plan. The prescription is 3 Gy x 5 fractions to 0.5cm depth. The vaginal mucosal Dmax is 158% of the prescription dose. The bladder D2cc is 4.2 Gy per fraction and the rectum D2cc is 3.8 Gy per fraction. Assess the plan quality and discuss the significance of the vaginal mucosal hotspot.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: vaginal cuff mucosal hotspot bladder rectum D2cc fraction dose

cases.input: For a rectal cancer interstitial brachytherapy boost case, evaluate the plan quality. The GTV D90 is 18.5 Gy in 3 fractions (total boost 18.5 Gy after 45Gy EBRT). The rectal D2cc is 5.5 Gy per fraction and the prostate Dmax is 4.8 Gy per fraction. Calculate the total EQD2 to the GTV and evaluate the OAR constraints.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: rectal boost GTV D90 EQD2 combined EBRT OAR constraint calculation

cases.input: Please evaluate the dose coverage for a head and neck interstitial brachytherapy case. The treatment volume is 12cc. The D90 is 5.8 Gy per fraction in 3 fractions. The D100 is 4.2 Gy per fraction. The mandible D2cc is 6.2 Gy per fraction. Evaluate the plan quality per Paris system criteria and discuss the clinical implications of the mandible dose.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: head neck interstitial Paris D90 D100 mandible D2cc

cases.input: I have a completed prostate brachytherapy plan that was evaluated on both CT and MRI. The CT-based D90 is 142 Gy and the MRI-based D90 is 148 Gy. The V100 on CT is 93% and on MRI is 95%. Evaluate the discrepancy between CT and MRI-based dosimetry and discuss which modality provides more reliable dose assessment for prostate brachytherapy.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: CT MRI prostate dosimetry D90 V100 discrepancy reliability comparison

cases.input: Please evaluate the dosimetric quality of a vaginal cancer interstitial brachytherapy plan. The HR-CTV D90 is 72 Gy EQD2. The bladder D2cc is 74 Gy EQD2, rectum D2cc is 68 Gy EQD2, and the sigmoid D2cc is 65 Gy EQD2. The patient received 45Gy EBRT prior to brachytherapy. Evaluate whether the plan meets GEC-ESTRO recommendations and if any OAR constraint is violated.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: vaginal interstitial HR-CTV D90 GEC-ESTRO OAR D2cc constraint violation

cases.input: For a penile cancer interstitial brachytherapy plan, evaluate the following: GTV D90 is 5.6 Gy per fraction in 3 fractions, the urethral Dmax is 5.8 Gy per fraction, and the penile shaft Dmax is 6.2 Gy per fraction. The GTV is 8cc. Evaluate the plan quality and discuss the clinical implications of urethral and penile shaft dose.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: penile GTV D90 urethral Dmax penile shaft dose fraction

cases.input: Please evaluate the dose distribution for a case of anal cancer brachytherapy boost. The GTV D90 is 15.5 Gy in 3 fractions. The anal sphincter Dmax is 16.8 Gy. The bladder D2cc is 8.2 Gy and the vaginal D2cc is 9.5 Gy (female patient). Calculate the total EQD2 for the GTV and all OARs, and assess plan quality.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: anal boost GTV D90 EQD2 anal sphincter bladder vaginal total dose

cases.input: I need to evaluate the isodose distribution for a prostate preplan. The 145 Gy isodose line covers 95% of the prostate, but there is a cold spot of 120 Gy in the left base region. The 200 Gy hot spot volume is 18% of the prostate. Evaluate the clinical significance of the cold spot and hot spot and recommend modifications.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: prostate preplan isodose cold spot hot spot 145 Gy 200 Gy modification

cases.input: For a nasopharyngeal intracavitary brachytherapy case, evaluate the plan. The nasopharyngeal mucosal D90 is 18 Gy in 3 fractions after 66Gy EBRT. The brainstem D2cc is 58 Gy EQD2. The right temporal lobe D2cc is 52 Gy EQD2. The optic chiasm Dmax is 54 Gy EQD2. Evaluate plan quality and identify any OAR constraints at risk.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: nasopharyngeal brainstem temporal lobe optic chiasm D2cc EQD2 tolerance

cases.input: Please evaluate the cumulative DVH for a patient who received prior pelvic EBRT (45Gy) and is now receiving HDR brachytherapy for a cervical cancer recurrence. The total cumulative doses are: bladder D2cc = 82 Gy EQD2, rectum D2cc = 75 Gy EQD2, sigmoid D2cc = 71 Gy EQD2. Assess whether these cumulative doses are within acceptable tolerance limits for re-irradiation.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: cumulative EQD2 cervical re-irradiation bladder rectum sigmoid tolerance

cases.input: I have a prostate brachytherapy post-implant scan with the following results: D90 = 138 Gy, V100 = 91%, V150 = 60%, urethral Dmax = 172 Gy (119% of Rx), rectal D1cc = 132 Gy. The patient reports significant urinary symptoms (IPSS score 24). Evaluate the dosimetric parameters in the context of the patient's symptoms and recommend clinical management.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: post-implant D90 V100 urethral Dmax IPSS urinary symptoms correlation

cases.input: For a vaginal cuff brachytherapy plan using a tandem applicator for a patient with vaginal melanoma, evaluate the dose distribution. The prescription is 3.5 Gy x 10 fractions. The vaginal mucosal Dmax is 5.2 Gy per fraction. The bladder D2cc is 3.0 Gy per fraction and rectum D2cc is 2.8 Gy per fraction. Assess plan quality for this radiosensitive tumor.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: vaginal melanoma mucosal hotspot OAR D2cc fraction radiosensitive

cases.input: Please evaluate the dose coverage for a liver interstitial brachytherapy case. The GTV D90 is 18 Gy in 2 fractions. The liver V15 is 28%, and the liver mean dose is 12 Gy. The right kidney Dmax is 8 Gy. The duodenal D2cc is 7.5 Gy. Evaluate plan quality and assess the risk of radiation-induced liver disease (RILD).
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: liver GTV D90 RILD risk liver V15 mean dose duodenal kidney

cases.input: Please evaluate the OAR doses for an anal cancer brachytherapy boost plan. The cumulative doses after EBRT (45Gy) and brachytherapy boost (15Gy) are: bladder D2cc = 55 Gy EQD2, rectum D2cc = 58 Gy EQD2, small bowel D2cc = 48 Gy EQD2, femoral heads Dmax = 42 Gy EQD2. Are these cumulative doses within safe tolerance limits?
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: anal cancer cumulative OAR EQD2 tolerance bladder rectum bowel femoral

cases.input: I have a breast APBI plan using a multi-channel catheter. The DVH shows PTV D90 = 32.5 Gy, V90 = 88%, Dmax = 37.2 Gy. The skin Dmax is 36.5 Gy (7mm from applicator). The rib Dmax is 34.8 Gy. Evaluate whether this plan meets ABS criteria and identify any areas of concern for potential toxicity.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: breast multi-channel APBI PTV D90 V90 skin rib Dmax ABS criteria

cases.input: For a head and neck cancer case, evaluate the dose to critical structures after interstitial brachytherapy boost. The cumulative EQD2 values are: spinal cord Dmax = 48 Gy, brainstem Dmax = 50 Gy, mandible D2cc = 72 Gy, bilateral parotids mean = 28 Gy (right) and 32 Gy (left). Assess these cumulative doses against published tolerance values and identify potential complications.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: head neck cumulative EQD2 spinal cord brainstem mandible parotid tolerance

cases.input: Please evaluate the dose homogeneity for a prostate preplan using stranded I-125 seeds. The homogeneity index (HI) is 0.42, the conformal index (CI) is 1.8, and the D90/V100 ratio is 1.03. The prostate volume is 40cc with 100 seeds loaded. Evaluate these plan quality metrics and compare them to published standards.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: prostate homogeneity index conformal index I-125 stranded D90 V100

cases.input: For a cervical cancer patient with a large HR-CTV volume (85cc) after EBRT, evaluate whether the brachytherapy plan achieves adequate coverage. The HR-CTV D90 is 78 Gy EQD2 (below the target of 87 Gy EQD2). The bladder D2cc is 82 Gy EQD2 (above the limit of 80). The rectum D2cc is 66 Gy EQD2. Evaluate the plan deficiencies and recommend strategies to improve coverage while respecting OAR constraints.
cases.description: Dose evaluation test: cervical HR-CTV D90 deficit bladder D2cc violation improvement strategy

cases.input: A prostate HDR brachytherapy plan has been generated with the following parameters: V100 of the prostate is 95%, V150 is 35%, the urethral Dmax is 115% of the prescribed dose, and the anterior rectal wall Dmax is 80% of the prescribed dose. Analyze this plan critically. What modifications would you recommend, and which dose-volume parameters are most concerning?
cases.description: Prostate HDR plan dose evaluation

cases.input: For a vaginal cylinder brachytherapy plan treating a vaginal cuff recurrence, the plan shows excellent coverage of the target with V100 = 98%, but the D2cc to the sigmoid is 65 Gy EQD2 and the D2cc to the bladder is 72 Gy EQD2. The patient also received 45 Gy EBRT to the pelvis. Evaluate whether these cumulative dose constraints are within acceptable limits and discuss any concerns.
cases.description: Cumulative dose constraint evaluation for vaginal brachytherapy

cases.input: A brachytherapy plan for a patient with recurrent anal cancer shows a highly conformal dose distribution but with a small cold spot at the posterior margin of the target volume. The cold spot represents approximately 5% of the PTV receiving less than 90% of the prescribed dose. Evaluate the clinical significance of this cold spot, discuss the potential impact on local control, and recommend whether replanning is indicated.
cases.description: Cold spot evaluation in brachytherapy plan

cases.input: For a PDR brachytherapy plan of a cervical cancer, the planned pulse schedule delivers 0.8 Gy per hour for 24 hours with a total of 19.2 Gy over the course. The tumor has an alpha-beta ratio of 10 and the rectum has an alpha-beta ratio of 3. Calculate the BED for both the tumor and rectum, and evaluate whether the therapeutic ratio is adequate. How would reducing the pulse interval affect the BED?
cases.description: BED calculation and evaluation for PDR brachytherapy

cases.input: Evaluate a brachytherapy plan for a pediatric patient with a nasopharyngeal rhabdomyosarcoma. The plan delivers 36 Gy in 12 fractions to the PTV, with D2cc to the brainstem of 42 Gy EQD2 and D2cc to the temporal lobes of 48 Gy EQD2. Given that this is a pediatric patient with long life expectancy, evaluate these dose constraints in the context of late effects risk and age-dependent tissue sensitivity.
cases.description: Pediatric brachytherapy dose evaluation

cases.input: A center has treated 50 cervical cancer patients with brachytherapy over the past year. Describe the process of performing a comprehensive dose audit for these patients. How would you analyze the dose-volume histogram data to identify trends, evaluate compliance with institutional dose constraints, correlate dose parameters with clinical outcomes, and present findings to the department?
cases.description: Comprehensive brachytherapy dose audit process

cases.input: A brachytherapy plan for a cervical cancer patient shows excellent target coverage (D90 = 85 Gy EQD2) but the D2cc to the rectum is 72 Gy EQD2, which is above the institutional tolerance of 70 Gy EQD2. The sigmoid D2cc is 58 Gy EQD2. Discuss the risk-benefit analysis for accepting this plan versus replanning to reduce rectal dose. What are the potential clinical consequences of the elevated rectal dose?
cases.description: Risk-benefit analysis for elevated rectal dose plan

cases.input: Evaluate the quality of an endometrial cancer vaginal cuff brachytherapy plan where the vaginal cylinder length is 4 cm, the prescribed dose is 21 Gy in 3 fractions, and the treatment depth at the vaginal surface is 5 mm. The D2cc to the rectum is 48 Gy EQD2 and the D2cc to the bladder is 52 Gy EQD2. Are these dose parameters adequate for tumor control while maintaining acceptable OAR doses?
cases.description: Vaginal cuff brachytherapy plan quality evaluation

cases.input: A brachytherapy plan for anal cancer uses a combination of endoluminal and interstitial applicators. The plan evaluation shows D90 = 58 Gy to the GTV, D90 = 52 Gy to the CTV, and the D2cc to the anal sphincter is 62 Gy. Evaluate the dose gradient between GTV and CTV, discuss whether the anal sphincter dose is within tolerance for maintaining continence, and recommend any plan modifications.
cases.description: Dose gradient and sphincter tolerance evaluation

cases.input: A brachytherapy plan for a vaginal cancer patient shows V100 of 96% for the target, but the dose distribution is highly inhomogeneous with a Dmax that is 180% of the prescribed dose. Discuss the clinical implications of the high dose maximum, the potential for necrosis at the high-dose region, and how this compares to the expected dose heterogeneity for this type of applicator.
cases.description: High dose maximum evaluation in vaginal brachytherapy

cases.input: Evaluate a brachytherapy plan for a patient with recurrent endometrial cancer where the vaginal apex is treated with a custom mold applicator. The prescription is 30 Gy in 5 fractions. The DVH shows D2cc rectum = 55 Gy EQD2, D2cc sigmoid = 48 Gy EQD2, and D2cc bladder = 60 Gy EQD2. The patient previously received 50 Gy EBRT with concurrent cisplatin. Discuss the cumulative dose constraints and whether the plan is safe to deliver.
cases.description: Cumulative dose safety evaluation

cases.input: For a patient with a large cervical tumor treated with MRI-guided adaptive brachytherapy, evaluate the adaptive plan across three treatment fractions. The HR-CTV D90 values are 86, 82, and 84 Gy EQD2 for fractions 1, 2, and 3 respectively. The rectum D2cc values are 65, 68, and 70 Gy EQD2. Analyze the trends in these dose parameters and discuss whether the increasing rectal dose across fractions is clinically significant.
cases.description: Dose parameter trend analysis across adaptive fractions

cases.input: Evaluate HDR plan for cervical cancer: tandem/ovoid with given dwell times. D90=89% of prescription, bladder D2cc=5.8 Gy, rectum D2cc=6.5 Gy. Is this acceptable? Recommend improvements.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: plan_eval_cervical

cases.input: Analyze cumulative doses at fraction 3 of 4. Projected final rectum D2cc=76 Gy EQD2 (exceeds 70 Gy limit). Recommend modifications for fraction 4.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: cumulative_fraction_modification

cases.input: Evaluate Day 0 post-implant CT for prostate seed implant: V100=91%, D90=108 Gy, urethra D10=118 Gy. Compare Day 0 vs expected Day 30 and discuss clinical significance.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: prostate_post_implant

cases.input: Analyze multi-catheter APBI plan: PTV V100=95%, V150=28%, D90=35.2 Gy, lung V20=12%, heart Dmax=4.8 Gy, skin D0.1cc=42 Gy. Evaluate per ABS/ESTRO guidelines.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: APBI_breast_plan

cases.input: Calculate BED and EQD2 for prostate HDR (15 needles, 38 Gy/4fx): prostate BED, urethra BED, rectum BED. Evaluate therapeutic ratio.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: HDR_prostate_biologic

cases.input: Evaluate VBT plan (30 Gy/5fx at 0.5 cm depth): mucosal surface dose 180%, D2cc values. Discuss prescription depth significance and mucosal tolerance.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: vaginal_cylinder_eval

cases.input: Analyze dose heterogeneity: D10=142%, D50=105%, D90=98%, D100=75%, DNR=1.85. Discuss clinical significance and improvement strategies.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: dose_heterogeneity_analysis

cases.input: Analyze cumulative doses at fraction 2. Projected rectum D2cc=78 Gy EQD2. Recommend fraction 3-4 modifications per EMBRACE constraints.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: cumulative_projection_analysis

cases.input: Evaluate 10-catheter APBI: PTV V100=92%, skin D0.1cc=38 Gy, rib D0.1cc=40 Gy. Discuss cosmetic outcome risks and long-term toxicities.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: APBI_interstitial_cosmesis

cases.input: Evaluate 12-needle vaginal interstitial plan (42 Gy/14fx BID): target V100=96%, urethra Dmax=110%. Discuss mucosal tolerance and fractionation.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: vaginal_interstitial_mucosal

cases.input: Evaluate three Pareto-optimal plans: Plan X (high coverage, high OAR), Plan Y (balanced), Plan Z (OAR-sparing, lower coverage). Select optimal plan.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: MCO_evaluation

cases.input: Evaluate QA results: source strength 0.8% difference, dwell position max 0.8 mm deviation, point dose 1.7% difference. Are these within TG-59 tolerance?
cases.description: Dose evaluation: QA_dosimetry_eval

cases.input: Calculate PDR BED for 30 Gy/60 pulses with 1-hour intervals (repair half-time 1.5 hours). Compare to equivalent HDR 38 Gy/4fx.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: PDR_BED_calculation

cases.input: Evaluate Day 30 post-implant: V100=96%, V150=48%, D90=112 Gy, urethra D10=122 Gy. Evaluate per ABS guidelines with clinical significance.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: prostate_seed_ABS_eval

cases.input: Evaluate combined EBRT 46 Gy + HDR 25 Gy boost: prostate D90 BED=285, urethra BED=245, rectum BED=168. Discuss therapeutic ratio and expected outcomes.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: combined_EBRT_HDR_BED

cases.input: Evaluate IGABT plan: HR-CTV D90=6.8 Gy (97%), GTV D90=7.5 Gy (107%). Discuss GTV dose escalation significance and adaptive vs non-adaptive.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: IGABT_adaptive_eval

cases.input: Evaluate interstitial plan for recurrent endometrial cancer (re-irradiation): target V100=94%, mucosal surface 149% of Rx. Discuss cumulative dose implications.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: re_irradiation_interstitial_eval

cases.input: Interpret DVH for cervical brachytherapy: D90=92% Rx, D2cc bladder=65 Gy, D2cc rectum=68 Gy, sigmoid D2cc=55 Gy. Evaluate against EMBRACE constraints.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: cervical_DVH_interpretation

cases.input: Analyze optimization tradeoffs: increasing D90 from 85 to 95 Gy raises rectum D2cc from 62 to 74 Gy. Discuss clinical decision-making.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: plan_optimization_tradeoffs

cases.input: Verify point dose: measured 7.15 Gy, planned 7.00 Gy (2.1% difference). Discuss tolerance, causes for discrepancy, and actions required.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: dose_calculation_verification

cases.input: Calculate total EQD2 for EBRT 45 Gy + HDR 7 Gy x4 + VBT 5 Gy x3 at point A. Include OAR cumulative doses.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: cumulative_VBT_HDR

cases.input: Evaluate seed implant quality: D90=115%, V100=97%, V150=45%, V200=12%, urethra D10=118%. Compare to ABS and ESTRO guidelines.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: prostate_seed_quality_indicators

cases.input: Calculate total treatment time and dose rate for HDR plan. Discuss whether it meets HDR classification.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: treatment_time_dose_rate

cases.input: Calculate Ir-192 source decay over 90 days from 8.5 Ci. Determine treatment time changes and replacement action level.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: source_decay_impact

cases.input: Analyze dwell time distribution for tandem/ovoid plan. Calculate total time, tandem/ovoid ratio, and transit time.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: dwell_time_distribution

cases.input: Calculate dose-rate at point A from dwell time distribution. Verify HDR classification.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: point_A_dose_calculation

cases.input: Calculate corrected air-kerma rate from well chamber measurement with temperature-pressure correction.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: well_chamber_measurement

cases.input: Calculate vaginal mucosal surface dose using inverse square law approximation. Evaluate against mucosal tolerance.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: vaginal_mucosa_dose

cases.input: Verify dwell positions from 2D diode array measurement. Calculate radial deviations and RMS.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: dwell_position_verification

cases.input: Calculate required lead shielding for HDR room with Ir-192. Include workload, distance, and HVL considerations.
cases.description: Dose evaluation: shielding_calculation

cases.input: I am designing a research study comparing TG-43 dose calculations with Monte Carlo simulations for a novel Cesium-131 prostate seed. The seed has a titanium shell with a ceramic CsI core. What specific dosimetric parameters should I compare between TG-43 and Monte Carlo? What are the expected sources of discrepancy? How should I report the differences in a peer-reviewed publication? Please reference relevant AAPM task group reports.
cases.description: Research methodology question comparing dose calculation methods

cases.input: I am implementing a brachytherapy treatment planning system from scratch. For the dose calculation engine, I need to implement the TG-43 formalism. Specifically, I need to calculate dose from an Ir-192 HDR source at an arbitrary point in 3D space. The source is modeled as a line source of active length 3.5 mm. Walk me through the step-by-step implementation of the geometry function for a line source, including the calculation of the angles theta_1 and theta_2, and the function G_L(r,theta).
cases.description: Implementation-level question about TG-43 geometry function calculation

cases.input: I am performing a TG-43 based dose calculation for a prostate LDR implant using I-125 seeds. The seeds are placed in a 3D pattern based on post-implant CT. The treatment planning system reports D90 of 165 Gy for the prostate. However, when I check the TG-43 data sheet for the seed model, the dose-rate constant is 0.965 cGy/(hr U). The total air-kerma strength of all seeds is 32.5 U. Can you verify whether the D90 calculation is reasonable by doing an approximate analytical check? What are the key assumptions in this verification?
cases.description: Dose verification question using TG-43 analytical methods

cases.input: I am comparing the dosimetric characteristics of three different HDR brachytherapy source models: Ir-192 (VariSource), Ir-192 (GammaMed), and Co-60 (BEBIG). I need to understand how the dose distribution differs between these sources due to their different energy spectra. Specifically: (1) How does the Co-60 energy (1.17 and 1.33 MeV) compare to Ir-192 (average 0.38 MeV) in terms of tissue penetration, (2) How do the anisotropy functions differ between these source types, and (3) What are the clinical implications of using Co-60 vs Ir-192 for HDR treatments?
cases.description: Comparative dosimetry question about different brachytherapy source types

cases.input: I need to calculate the biological effective dose (BED) for an HDR brachytherapy treatment of cervical cancer. The treatment delivers 6 Gy per fraction x 5 fractions (total 30 Gy) to point A. The tumor alpha/beta ratio is 10 Gy and the repair half-time for late effects is 1.5 hours. Using the linear-quadratic model with incomplete repair, calculate: (1) the BED for the tumor, (2) the BED for late effects with alpha/beta = 3 Gy, and (3) the EQD2 for both tumor and late effects. Show your calculations.
cases.description: Detailed BED calculation for HDR brachytherapy

cases.input: I am implementing a Monte Carlo dose calculation for a novel brachytherapy source. The source has a complex geometry with multiple radioactive layers and a tungsten marker. I need to understand: (1) What specific physics models should I use in MCNP or Geant4 for photon transport at brachytherapy energies, (2) How should I model the source geometry including the radioactive core, encapsulation, and marker, (3) What statistical convergence criteria should I use for the dose scoring tally, and (4) How should I validate my Monte Carlo simulation against experimental measurements?
cases.description: Advanced Monte Carlo implementation question for brachytherapy dose calculation

cases.input: I am reviewing a brachytherapy plan for cervical cancer using a tandem and ovoid applicator. The plan shows point A dose of 6.0 Gy per fraction, with the following OAR doses: rectal point 4.2 Gy, bladder point 4.8 Gy. The prescription is for 5 fractions. I want to understand the TG-43 uncertainty budget for this plan. What are the main sources of uncertainty in the dose calculation? How should I combine the individual uncertainties? What is the total uncertainty at the 95% confidence level?
cases.description: Detailed uncertainty analysis question for brachytherapy dose calculation

cases.input: I need to compare the radial dose function g(r) for three different I-125 seed models: model 6711, model 2301, and model 1251. How do the radial dose functions differ between these seed models? What physical factors cause these differences? How do these differences affect clinical dose distributions for prostate implants? I need this comparison for a quality assurance study comparing different seed models.
cases.description: Comparative dosimetry question about I-125 seed models

cases.input: I need to calculate the TG-43 dose distribution for a tandem and ovoid applicator used in cervical cancer brachytherapy. The tandem has an active length of 6 cm, and the ovoids have a diameter of 2 cm each. For a point at the left ovoid surface (the leftmost ovoid reference point), how do I calculate the dose contribution from: (1) the tandem source, (2) the left ovoid source, and (3) the right ovoid source? What is the total dose at this point?
cases.description: TG-43 application question for tandem and ovoid dose calculation

cases.input: I am studying the effect of radioactive source decay on brachytherapy dose delivery. For Ir-192 with a half-life of 73.83 days, if I calibrate a source on January 1 and plan to use it for treatments throughout the year, how does the source strength change over time? Calculate the source strength remaining at 3 months, 6 months, and 12 months. If I need a minimum dose-rate of 5 Gy/hr for HDR treatments, what is the practical useful life of the source?
cases.description: Question about radioactive decay effects on brachytherapy source strength

cases.input: I need to understand the TG-138 report on dose calculation accuracy in brachytherapy. What are the specific recommendations in TG-138 regarding: (1) when model-based dose calculations should be used instead of TG-43, (2) what level of dose calculation accuracy is required for different clinical scenarios, (3) how to validate model-based calculations against measurements, and (4) what clinical scenarios show the largest discrepancies between TG-43 and model-based calculations?
cases.description: Detailed question about AAPM TG-138 dose calculation accuracy recommendations

cases.input: I need to calculate the dose distribution for a multi-catheter breast brachytherapy applicator. The applicator has 15 catheters with a total of 85 dwell positions. The prescription dose is 3.4 Gy per fraction x 10 fractions to a 1 cm margin around the applicator. Using TG-43 formalism, how would I approach the dose optimization for this plan? What are the key considerations for dwell time optimization? How do I ensure adequate coverage of the target volume while minimizing hot spots?
cases.description: Question about multi-catheter breast brachytherapy planning optimization

cases.input: I am performing a TG-43 based dose calculation for a cervical cancer brachytherapy plan. I want to calculate the dose at an arbitrary point in 3D space due to a single dwell position in the tandem. The dwell position is at coordinates (0, 0, 0) in the applicator coordinate system. The point of interest is at (2.0, 1.5, 0.8) cm from the dwell position. Given the TG-43 parameters for the Ir-192 source (dose-rate constant = 1.108 cGy/(hr U), geometry function, radial dose function, and anisotropy function at this distance and angle), calculate the dose rate at this point.
cases.description: Step-by-step TG-43 dose calculation demonstration

cases.input: I am performing a dose calculation for a prostate LDR implant using Pd-103 seeds. I need to understand the biological effective dose (BED) comparison between Pd-103 and I-125 implants. Given that Pd-103 has a half-life of 17 days versus 59.4 days for I-125, how does the dose-rate effect affect the biological effectiveness? What alpha/beta ratio should I use for prostate cancer? How do the BED values compare for equivalent physical dose prescriptions?
cases.description: Radiobiology question comparing Pd-103 and I-125 biological effectiveness

cases.input: I am performing a dose calculation comparison between a brachytherapy TPS and an independent dose calculation program. The TPS uses TG-43 dose calculation, and I want to verify the dose at the prescription point. How should I set up the independent dose calculation? What TG-43 parameters should I use? What is the acceptable tolerance between the TPS and independent calculation? How do I document the verification?
cases.description: Practical question about independent dose calculation verification

cases.input: I need to understand the TG-91 report on the clinical use of LDR prostate brachytherapy. What are the specific recommendations in TG-91 regarding: (1) patient selection criteria, (2) treatment planning methodology, (3) intraoperative quality assurance, (4) post-implant dosimetry evaluation, and (5) follow-up protocols? How do the TG-91 recommendations compare with current clinical practice?
cases.description: Comprehensive question about AAPM TG-91 LDR prostate brachytherapy recommendations
